extracted_rules: This indicator can be used for any type of trading strategies whether it is trend following strategy, reversal strategy or breakout trading strategy because it gives you a signal when there is a sudden increase of momentum in the market.

So depending on the size of the bar if the bar passes the alert level or impulse level we can have a signal about increase of momentum.

when the bar passes the alert level the dashed line it is alert signal that the momentum is probably going to increase and when the bar passes the impulse level the solid line here it is impulse signal and it tells us that we had a sudden increase of momentum in the market but not any impulse signal means that the price is going to further move in that direction for example for this impulse signal that we had here which is a downside signal you can see that the price didn't go down considerably.
But deciding on if the context confirms further movement of the price after that needs more analysis, which I explain it now.
So, as I said, when we have impulse signal, it only tells us that in that time period, we had considerable increase of momentum.

Two of the main situations where you may get a false signal like this is when a reversal is happening in the market like this case here and another one is when a pullback happens in the market especially deeper pullbacks.
During this downtrend when we identify that the current trend is down when we got a bearish signal like this.
So you must have a reversal identification in your system even when you're using a trend following strategy so you can avoid false signals like this where a reversal is happening in the market.
This one relative strength index and see if RSI is showing any divergence or not.
You can see that following this downtrend where the price was making lower highs and lower lows here when the price made lower low you can see that RSI made higher low.
So at this point when we got this bearish signal we don't enter a trade because there is a probability of reversal in the market.
You can see that when this signal happened the price couldn't break the previous low and here tight consolidation.
For a bullish divergence, we mark the high of the pattern and we wait to see if the price can break above this high or not.
Because if this high is broken, which is the last lower high following this downtrend, it tells us that the structure of the downtrend is already broken.
So the reversal here is confirmed and in practice we could enter a long position at this point.
When the MACD histogram is above the center line and green, the market is in an uptrend.
And when the MACD histogram is below the center line and red, the market is in a downtrend.

So when MACD histogram is green, the focus is on bullish signals.
